Title: Traveling Salesman — City / Neighborhood / Address Semantic Geometry
Statement: Given a finite city-scale set of required addresses and explicit symmetric travel costs, represent the graph, supplied tours, inference state, and residual search as executable structural data.
Scope: Initial scope is one finite symmetric complete weighted graph with a fixed depot, five required addresses, explicit canonical undirected edge rows, and supplied ordered tour witnesses. The scope validates tours; it does not yet find or prove an optimal tour.
TrustBoundary: InputGraphKernel: city/neighborhood/address membership, required-stop selection, and TravelEdges.DistanceMeters/TravelCost are trusted input data. Formula semantics are projected into Postgres and independently replayed by the Python reference substrate.

Loops.Result: City -> Neighborhood -> Address hierarchy closed for the fixture.
Five-stop complete symmetric weighted graph normalized as ten canonical edges.
Candidate tours represented as ordered stops plus edge-bound legs.
Valid reference route accepted; duplicate-stop route rejected; optimality remains false.
Baseline records twelve symmetry-reduced route classes and zero semantic elimination.

CurrentCertificates.Conclusion: tsp-gridville-5 has five stops and ten unique canonical undirected edges.
A-B-C-D-E-A is a valid supplied tour of cost 14 within the declared instance.
A-B-C-B-E-A is rejected because B is visited twice and D is omitted.

RemainingFrontier: symmetry-class collapse beyond fixed depot and reversal
subtour exclusion certificates for unordered edge-set candidates
branching and backtracking only after constraint closure
